stimulus: “Stanzas Composed on a September Afternoon” is an 1822 poem by Helena Carstairs. In the poem, the speaker describes having mixed feelings while taking in the sights and sounds of an autumn day: ______blank
stem: Which quotation from “Stanzas Composed on a September Afternoon” most effectively illustrates the claim?

answerOptions.content: “Through hawthorn-hedges, in that quiet lane, / The bramble-bine flung out its braid; / And ’tis my faith that every grain / Enjoys the light that does not fade.”
“The yellowed leaves now spread their fan, / To catch the harvest air; / And I must think, do all I can, / That there was gladness there.”
“The starlings hopp’d and play’d a tune: / Their thoughts I cannot measure, / But the least chirrup they made at noon, / It seem’d a thrill of pleasure.”
“I heard a thousand mingled notes, / While by an oak I [sat] reclined, / In that sweet mood when pleasant thoughts / Bring heavy thoughts to the mind.”

rationale: Choice D is the best answer because the quotation shows the speaker experiencing “pleasant thoughts” and “heavy thoughts” together while listening to autumn sounds—directly illustrating mixed feelings while taking in the sights and sounds of an autumn day. Choice A is incorrect because the quotation expresses an unmixed faith that the grain enjoys the light. Choice B is incorrect because the speaker is determinedly imagining gladness rather than reporting mixed feelings. Choice C is incorrect because the quotation suggests the speaker attributes pleasure to the starlings without describing the speaker’s own mixed feelings.
